tweets.text:
America needs vetted and legal farm workers to support our local agricultural businesses, and we’re working closely with @USDOL to modernize and streamline the H-2A process. 
 
Beginning Oct. 2, petitioners seeking unnamed beneficiaries will be able to electronically submit the newly developed Form I-129H2A after DOL issues a notice of acceptance on the H-2A temporary labor certification and before it is approved. USCIS does not approve any petitions until after DOL has approved the corresponding TLC. This change will allow USCIS to immediately begin the concurrent processing of electronically filed petitions with unnamed beneficiaries, allowing petitioners the flexibility to file a petition with USCIS sooner than previously permitted. 
 
When workers choose lawful pathways to working in the United States, it is beneficial for American businesses, the public, and alien workers. 
 
Learn more: https://t.co/x00OdmSSz7
